event: computed tomography of chest revealed septic pulmonary emboli
decision to proceed with tricuspid valvulectomy
blood cultures confirmed methicillin-susceptible S aureus
valve cultures confirmed methicillin-susceptible S aureus
partial pressure of oxygen 60 mm Hg
chest radiography showed no pulmonary edema
chest radiography showed no lung consolidation
partial pressure of oxygen 145–165 mm Hg
right common femoral vein cannula (21 Fr)
ECMO specialty transport team retrieved patient
wide-open systolic regurgitant flow from right ventricle to right atrium
interatrial septum bowing right to left
outflow jet directed into right atrium near interatrial septum
taken to operating room for PFO closure
TV replacement with 31-mm St. Jude Epic porcine bioprosthetic valve
intraoperative TEE showed no residual tricuspid regurgitation
no residual right-to-left shunt by bubble study
remained in hospital for additional 19 days
